quoted_tweet.text:
$RKLB Busy week for me. Did I miss an announcement on this? Do we have confirmation? Seems huge. 🚨🚨🚨

“Meta Announces Space-Based Solar Power Partnership with Rocket Lab”

“Meta has signed a strategic partnership with Rocket Lab to deploy a network of solar power satellites in low Earth orbit, aiming to commercialize space-based solar energy by 2030. With an initial investment of $1 billion, the project will leverage Rocket Lab’s launch capabilities and satellite manufacturing technology, combined with Meta’s AI energy management systems, to explore the feasibility of transmitting clean electricity back to Earth.”

kept.text:
🐋 $RKLB Whale Watch

$100 calls just went near-vertical to $4.5M while price sits at $78. The $65 anchor is holding at $4.5M — already in the money. $25M+ stacked across ten call strikes, one recovering put block that's fading, not pressing.

Whales are buying into May 7 earnings. Cathie Wood just sold. One of them is wrong.

Which side are you on into the print? 👇

profile_bio.description: I enjoy drinking good wine as much as I do trading stocks; they also happen to both cost me money.

quoted_tweet.text:
$RKLB (Algo Flow) - Algos finding a bottom?

Last week, net options activity fell off a cliff. Flow dropped from near zero all the way to -4M while price slid from $92 to $78. By Friday, both were recovering — flow climbed back to -2.5M and price bounced to $82. The selling slowed. It hasn't stopped.

Here's the important part: Flow is still well below zero and that's the line that matters. The bounce is real but unconfirmed. 

Catalyst stack remains strong: CEO cut salary to $1, Neutron launch permit filed for H2 2026, $190M DoD hypersonic contract.

Bulls watch: flow continuing to climb toward zero — if it crosses above with price reclaiming $87+, the bearish read flips entirely.

profile_bio.description: I enjoy drinking good wine as much as I do trading stocks; they also happen to both cost me money.

kept.text: $ASTS $RKLB Part of my thesis is that US GOV space defense spent will to a degree move from the bespoke systems built by Rocket Lab and the primes to commercial solutions from SpaceX, Amazon and AST. Not final yet, but its quite clear the Space Force wants to cancel the SDA Transport Layer. Again not everything will be commercial solutions, there will always be room for bespoke, but the high margin stuff is commercial solutions. Again broader picture is space budget will increase significantly so all will win. My thesis is that commercial solutions will increasing capture a bigger part of that pie. That’s why I am so bullish on AST defense.

profile_bio.description: Conquering markets instead of empires. 🏰📈 Buying stocks like they're Persian territories, HODLing like it's 323 BC!

quoted_tweet.text:
The pivot from the Space Development Agency's (SDA) bespoke Tranches to the Space Data Network (SDN) is a massive tailwind for $ASTS. By killing "T3" in favor of SDN, the Space Force is officially signaling a shift from "gov-spec" builds to commercial-first hybrid architectures
